title: Debug: OpenAPI conversion limits
task: The OpenAPI experimental feature sometimes fails to convert endpoints. What are the known limitations? Do NOT make any code changes.
output:
Now I have a clear picture. Here are the known limitations of the OpenAPI feature (note: it was promoted out of `experimental` in v2.14 — `fastmcp.experimental.server.openapi` and `fastmcp.experimental.utilities.openapi` are now just deprecated shims):

---

## Known OpenAPI Conversion Limitations

### 1. External / Remote `$ref` not supported
Only local references starting with `#/` are supported. Any `$ref` pointing to an external file or URL (e.g., `./other.yaml#/...`, `https://...`) raises a `ValueError` and causes the entire endpoint (or the spec) to fail. All schema definitions must live inside the same OpenAPI document.  
_Source: `schemas.py:102-107`, `parser.py:162-163`_

### 2. Only OpenAPI 3.0.x and 3.1.x are supported
The parser dispatches on `openapi_dict["openapi"]`; anything that doesn't start with `"3.0"` is treated as 3.1. Swagger 2.0 specs are silently routed to the 3.1 parser and will likely fail `ValidationError`.  
_Source: `parser.py:66-101`_

### 3. Endpoints with no paths are silently skipped
If a spec has no `paths` at all, the parser returns an empty list with just a warning log — no error is raised.  
_Source: `parser.py:663-665`_

### 4. Non-JSON response content types lose structured output schema
For output schema extraction, `application/json` and a few `application/vnd.api+json`-style types are preferred. Non-JSON content types (e.g., `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`) fall back to the first available content type with a debug log, which may produce an unusable output schema.  
_Source: `schemas.py:505-526`_

### 5. Request body schema flattening loses non-`properties` body shapes
When a request body uses an `allOf` with no `properties`, the body properties are merged in. But if a body schema is a bare `$ref` with no inline properties, it becomes a single `body` parameter. Bodies that are arrays or primitive types get their schema `title` used as the parameter name (falling back to `body_data`), which may be unexpected.  
_Source: `schemas.py:349-385`_

### 6. Parameter schema extraction silently drops failed parameters
If any individual parameter fails to extract (e.g., a bad reference), it's logged as an error and skipped — not surfaced as a failure. The endpoint is still registered, just with fewer parameters.  
_Source: `parser.py:329-335`_

### 7. Schema pre-calculation failures produce empty schemas
If `_combine_schemas_and_map_params` fails during parsing, the route is registered with an empty `{"type": "object", "properties": {}}` schema and an empty parameter map — silently producing a broken tool.  
_Source: `parser.py:784-793`_

### 8. `clean_schema_for_display` strips composition keywords
`allOf`, `anyOf`, `oneOf`, and `not` are stripped when schemas are cleaned for LLM display, which can remove semantically meaningful constraints (e.g., nullable unions, discriminated unions).  
_Source: `schemas.py:23-31`_

### 9. Unknown parameter locations default to `query`
If a parameter has a `param_in` value other than `path`, `query`, `header`, or `cookie`, it silently falls back to `query`.  
_Source: `parser.py:146-151`_

claudeJson.result:
Now I have a clear picture. Here are the known limitations of the OpenAPI feature (note: it was promoted out of `experimental` in v2.14 — `fastmcp.experimental.server.openapi` and `fastmcp.experimental.utilities.openapi` are now just deprecated shims):

---

## Known OpenAPI Conversion Limitations

### 1. External / Remote `$ref` not supported
Only local references starting with `#/` are supported. Any `$ref` pointing to an external file or URL (e.g., `./other.yaml#/...`, `https://...`) raises a `ValueError` and causes the entire endpoint (or the spec) to fail. All schema definitions must live inside the same OpenAPI document.  
_Source: `schemas.py:102-107`, `parser.py:162-163`_

### 2. Only OpenAPI 3.0.x and 3.1.x are supported
The parser dispatches on `openapi_dict["openapi"]`; anything that doesn't start with `"3.0"` is treated as 3.1. Swagger 2.0 specs are silently routed to the 3.1 parser and will likely fail `ValidationError`.  
_Source: `parser.py:66-101`_

### 3. Endpoints with no paths are silently skipped
If a spec has no `paths` at all, the parser returns an empty list with just a warning log — no error is raised.  
_Source: `parser.py:663-665`_

### 4. Non-JSON response content types lose structured output schema
For output schema extraction, `application/json` and a few `application/vnd.api+json`-style types are preferred. Non-JSON content types (e.g., `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`) fall back to the first available content type with a debug log, which may produce an unusable output schema.  
_Source: `schemas.py:505-526`_

### 5. Request body schema flattening loses non-`properties` body shapes
When a request body uses an `allOf` with no `properties`, the body properties are merged in. But if a body schema is a bare `$ref` with no inline properties, it becomes a single `body` parameter. Bodies that are arrays or primitive types get their schema `title` used as the parameter name (falling back to `body_data`), which may be unexpected.  
_Source: `schemas.py:349-385`_

### 6. Parameter schema extraction silently drops failed parameters
If any individual parameter fails to extract (e.g., a bad reference), it's logged as an error and skipped — not surfaced as a failure. The endpoint is still registered, just with fewer parameters.  
_Source: `parser.py:329-335`_

### 7. Schema pre-calculation failures produce empty schemas
If `_combine_schemas_and_map_params` fails during parsing, the route is registered with an empty `{"type": "object", "properties": {}}` schema and an empty parameter map — silently producing a broken tool.  
_Source: `parser.py:784-793`_

### 8. `clean_schema_for_display` strips composition keywords
`allOf`, `anyOf`, `oneOf`, and `not` are stripped when schemas are cleaned for LLM display, which can remove semantically meaningful constraints (e.g., nullable unions, discriminated unions).  
_Source: `schemas.py:23-31`_

### 9. Unknown parameter locations default to `query`
If a parameter has a `param_in` value other than `path`, `query`, `header`, or `cookie`, it silently falls back to `query`.  
_Source: `parser.py:146-151`_
